perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Understanding, the term "associates" or "relationships" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term "contacts" to "relationships" creates an in-text contradiction, particularly because the term "contacts" is used elsewhere in the agreement. Now, there is confusion whether contacts are to be defined the same as relationships, or if they're regarded as different definitions all together. Now it's not clear whether the non-circumvention clause also covers 'relationships' in the same scope as 'contacts.' The in-text contradiction appears because other references to 'contacts' are not updated to 'relationships.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party, for itself and its associates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "associates" or "contacts". The changed text redefines this to "associates" or "relationships". The contradicted text uses "contacts" as a defined term. By changing "contacts" to "relationships" in the definition, but not in the contradicted text, it creates uncertainty whether "contacts" in the contradicted text is covered by the new definition, or if it's treated separately. This directly contradicts the expectation of a consistent definition and creates an in-text contradiction as it now is unclear whether the non-circumvention clause also covers 'relationships' in the same scope as 'contacts.'

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the <PAGE> 2 principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contracts" and or "Associates".
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the <PAGE> 2 principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contacts" and or "Affiliates".
perturbation.explanation: The original section defines that the Parties learn from each other lenders, agents, brokers, clients as 'Contracts' or 'Associates'. Interchanging 'Associates' to 'Affiliates' introduces an in-text contradiction. This is because, in the previous sections, 'Associates' were previously defined, and now the term is being swaped with 'Affiliates', which the definition for 'Associates' doesn't apply to. So this leads to an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party, for itself and its associates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Contracts' or 'Associates' as lenders, agents, brokers, clients. The changed text alters 'Associates' to 'Affiliates'. The contradicted text refers to 'associates as defined below'. Since 'Associates' were replaced with 'Affiliates' in the changed text, but the contradicted text expects the original definition of 'Associates' (lenders, agents, brokers, clients) to apply, there is now a contradiction. The 'associates as defined below' in the Contradicted Text no longer refers to the original definition, as the original definition has been replaced in the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that the "Contracts" of each party hereto are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable "Contracts" and that the parties will not directly or indirectly negotiate or participate in any transaction circumventing the party who first provided the "Contract".
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that the "Deals" of each party hereto are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable "Deals" and that the parties will not directly or indirectly negotiate or participate in any transaction circumventing the party who first provided the "Deal".
perturbation.explanation: Here, interchanging the word 'Contracts' to 'Deals' will confuse the initial terminology being used. Because 'Contracts' and 'Associates' are defined in the previous paragraph as "Contracts" and or "Associates". When swapping the original 'Contracts' to 'Deals', this becomes contradictory, because if we make the assumption that 'Deals' and 'Contracts' can be used interchangeably, then that definition we just mentioned would apply here and be contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the <PAGE> 2 principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contracts" and or "Associates".
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term 'Contracts', which is explicitly defined in the contradicted text as a list of contacts ('Contracts' and or 'Associates'). The changed text replaces 'Contracts' with 'Deals'. This creates a contradiction because 'Deals' is not defined in the contract, and replacing 'Contracts' with 'Deals' means the provision regarding non-circumvention no longer applies to the defined 'Contracts' (i.e., the list of contacts), causing an inconsistency. The explanation given supports that this change introduces a terminology contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party represents, warrants and covenants that all information furnished by said party, or to be furnished by said Party, or to any other Party or Parties hereto is, or will be, true, complete, correct and accurate to best of said Party's knowledge, ability and belief.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party represents, warrants and covenants that all information furnished by said entity, or to be furnished by said Entity, or to any other Party or Parties hereto is, or will be, true, complete, correct and accurate to best of said Party's knowledge, ability and belief.
perturbation.explanation: Swapping the word 'party' for 'entity', will create a contradiction. In the document, there is a 'Party' defined as High Speed Net Solutions and R. J. Seifert Enterprises. However, if we start swapping party for entity, it won't be as clear who that definition is referencing. This affects legal interpretation of which parties are being referenced, creating uncertainty, as 'entity' is undefined, introducing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Circumvention and Non-Disclosure Agreement (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement" is made this 6th of February, 1999 by and between High Speed Net Solutions and R. J. Seifert Enterprises. Collectively, all the parties hereto may be referred to hereinafter as the "Parties", shall include both disclosing party and informed party without prejudice.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Party', which is a defined term within the contract, specifically High Speed Net Solutions and R. J. Seifert Enterprises as seen in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'entity', which is undefined. This creates ambiguity and introduces a contradiction because the contradicted text defines 'Parties' as High Speed Net Solutions and R. J. Seifert Enterprises, making it unclear to whom the obligations now apply if 'entity' is used, leading to a Misaligned Terminology and thus an in-text contradiction as the definition of 'Parties' is no longer clearly referenced.
